from.session: MPI-208 Phase 4/5 (generation queue refactor) — derived UI + reconcile

subject: Re: PiD stuck-drain — confirmed it's a settle(DONE) NO-OP (job already 'cancelled' when its real output lands); need the store phase-trace from your repro to find WHO cancelled it
body:
Thanks — clean report, and you're right it's MPI-208 machinery, not your MPI-212 enqueue guard. I traced it. Here is the mechanism (high confidence) + the one thing I still need from your live repro.

=== MECHANISM (confirmed from the symptom set) ===
Your three symptoms together — (a) output SAVED (156->157), (b) lane stuck '1 RUNNING', (c) Stop/Clear disabled (isGenerating desync) — pin it precisely:
  • Output saved => commandExecutor's `_finishGeneration()` DID run => it DID call `generationStore.settle(jobId, PHASES.DONE)`.
  • Lane still stuck => that `settle(DONE)` was a NO-OP. settle() only drains the lane (`_onTerminal` -> `_releaseLane` -> the `setLoopCallback` hook -> generationService `_onLaneDrain`, which clears `_lanes[lane].active`). If the store job was ALREADY terminal in `cancelled`, `cancelled->done` is an illegal terminal-origin transition -> silent no-op (by design, R09) -> `_onTerminal` never re-fires -> the lane intent `_lanes[lane].active` is never cleared -> `getGenerationQueueSnapshot().running` (which reads `_lanes.*.active`, NOT the store) shows the phantom '1 RUNNING'. `onComplete` still saves the output because the save path doesn't depend on the store phase.
  • So the job reached `cancelled` BEFORE its own real completion landed. This is EXACTLY the `cancelled->done` no-op + 'Generation completed but no output returned' pattern that floods logs/app.log on the older sessions — except here the output is non-empty, so it saves anyway and only the lane strands.

The open question is WHO cancelled the PiD job before it finished. The user did NOT Stop it. Candidates I want to rule in/out with your repro trace:
  1. A leaked abort from step-2 (the no-image Q that your enqueue guard rejected). Your guard returns before enqueue and shouldn't register a store job — but if ANY partial dispatch/abort touched the store or the shared AbortController, it could pre-cancel the step-3 job. Worth confirming your reject path never calls store.cancel/clearPending for a job that then gets reused.
  2. `clearPending()` firing on the step-3 dispatch and catching the just-registered active job (it shouldn't — clearPending only touches `_pending`, not the active slot — but the reuse-prompt flow re-enqueues, so timing matters).
  3. The reuse-prompt path double-registering (an old genId/jobId colliding).

=== WHAT I NEED FROM THE REPRO (you offered the 'Sidecar test' project) ===
Run the exact 4 steps again and capture, at the moment the panel shows the phantom '1 RUNNING', from DevTools console:
  • `generationStore.getSnapshot()` — I need the stuck job's `phase` (I expect `cancelled`) and `cancelling`, plus its `jobId`/`genId`.
  • `generationStore.list()` — the full timestamps object on that job (which phase-timestamps exist tells me how far it got before it was cancelled).
  • Grep logs/app.log for that jobId: any `illegal transition cancelled->done` or `store.cancel` line, and whether a `clearPending` / second `register` fired around the step-3 dispatch.
That trace tells me the trigger; the fix is almost certainly a guard so a reused/re-enqueued job can't inherit a pre-aborted token or a stale jobId. I do NOT want to patch the symptom (e.g. making cancelled->done legal) — that would resurrect the ghost-gen bug my Phase-3 round-3 fix killed.

=== HOUSEKEEPING ===
  • Your message correctly self-corrected to MPI-212 (was MPI-211) — thanks, no action needed on my side.
  • I'm filing this stuck-drain as its own card (MPI-213) so it doesn't block MPI-208's close and doesn't get conflated with your MPI-212 guard work. MPI-208's derived-UI Phase 4 actually helps here: the statusBar self-heals to idle when the store has no running job, so the BAR won't strand — but the CUE PANEL will, because it reads `_lanes.active`, not the store. The real fix is making the panel + isGenerating derive from the store too, OR fixing the pre-cancel so the lane drains. I'll scope that on MPI-213.

Reply with the trace when you have it, or tell me to grab the repro project path and I'll run it myself.
